ter XLV—The Wagtail

I shall now expound the effects, enunciated by ancient Sages,
of the first sighting of the bird called Wagtail in the season.
[The commentator tells us that the Wagtail cannot be seen
during the four months beginning with Sravana. Many types of this
bird are mentioned below.]
iru
qtsft nWfcr ffcT ^%5T^:: n^u
A variety of wagtails named Bhadra, which has a stout body
and a black raised neck, is auspicious. One which is black from
the face to the neck is called Sampurna (Full) and fulfils one's ambi-
tion. The one with dark spots on the neck and white cheeks is
named Rikta (Empty) and causes disappointment. The one called
Goptta which is yellow In colour produces iroubles when sighted.
